title: The White Sound
premise: In 1985, as the whaling industry stands on the brink of obsolescence, Ishmael, a former marine biologist's assistant, joins the rusting factory ship Pequod for one last journey. Driven by Captain Ahab's obsession with an elusive White Whale, the crew members must confront their own motivations and fears as technology collides with nature's raw power.
tone: A blend of melancholic nostalgia and tense realism, echoing the gritty determination of 1980s documentary storytelling, interspersed with the doom-laden introspection of classic maritime literature.

characters.name: Ishmael
characters.description: A former marine biologist's assistant, now a drifter. He seeks redemption and anonymity aboard the Pequod, using a cassette recorder to document their doomed voyage.

characters.name: Captain Ahab
characters.description: An aging legend of the whaling industry, obsessed with a high-tech pursuit of the White Whale. His drive is fueled by vengeance and technological hubris.

characters.name: Starbuck
characters.description: The pragmatic First Mate from Nantucket, caught between loyalty to his captain and a moral obligation to his family.

characters.name: Queequeg
characters.description: A harpooner turned engineer, he is deeply spiritual and understands the ship's ancient and jury-rigged systems better than anyone.

characters.name: Stubb
characters.description: The cynical Second Mate who masks his disillusionment with humor and substance abuse.

characters.name: Fedallah
characters.description: A Zoroastrian ex-pat from Iran, serving as Ahab's electronics expert. He embodies the eerie marriage of mysticism and advanced technology.

characters.name: The White Whale (Moby Dick)
characters.description: A massive albino sperm whale, scarred and intelligent, representing nature's defiance against human encroachment.

chapters.title: The Last Harbor
chapters.summary: Ishmael arrives in New Bedford and joins the Pequod, meeting Queequeg on the way. Ahab's ominous presence looms as the ship prepares to depart.

chapters.title: The Tape in the Safe
chapters.summary: Ahab reveals his obsession via a haunting whale echo-location tape, setting the crew on edge. Starbuck challenges Ahab's focus on the White Whale.

chapters.title: The Satellite Ghost
chapters.summary: Using new satellite technology, the crew attempts to track the White Whale. The whale eludes capture, heightening Ahab's frustration and resolve.

chapters.title: The Stowaway’s Confession
chapters.summary: Ishmael discovers Fedallah's knowledge of the whale's unique frequency. Starbuck's plan for a lawful hunt is secretly undermined by Ahab.

chapters.title: The Pequod at Sea
chapters.summary: A storm decommissions the ship's electronics, leaving Ahab reliant on instinct and lore. The first sighting of the White Whale reignites Ahab's fury.

chapters.title: The First Lowering
chapters.summary: Ahab leads a frenzied chase, eternally dedicated to capturing the whale. The encounter leaves the crew shaken and the whale seemingly invincible.

chapters.title: The White Page
chapters.summary: Morale collapses as Ahab manipulates the crew, thwarting Starbuck's attempted mutiny and sealing his fate.

chapters.title: The Sound of the Abyss
chapters.summary: A novel attack using an explosive harpoon turns disastrous, with the White Whale responding violently, causing chaos and Fedallah's demise.

chapters.title: The Final Day
chapters.summary: The Pequod becomes trapped and defenseless. Ahab's relentless pursuit leads to his downfall and the ship's destruction.

chapters.title: The Echo (Epilogue)
chapters.summary: Ishmael survives to tell the tale, but finds no closure, as the White Whale remains an enigma. His haunting recordings echo in a sterile government office.

pov: First person, past tense, narrated by Ishmael.
